summary: The merchant loses his house, ships, goods, and wealth, and his family retreats to a poor cottage in the forest.
When the merchant leaves to investigate a ship, Beauty asks only for his safe return and, when pressed, for a rose.
Lost in snow, the merchant finds a splendid empty castle where food, warmth, and shelter appear for him.
The Beast catches the merchant taking a rose and agrees to spare him if one daughter willingly comes to the castle.
After hearing the bargain, Beauty chooses to return with her father and stay with the Beast.
Beauty arrives at the palace, confirms she came willingly, and the Beast sends her father home.
Beauty dreams of a handsome Prince and a stately lady who warn her not to trust appearances.
The Beast visits Beauty each evening and repeatedly asks if she will marry him, but she refuses at first.
Beauty longs for home, and the Beast lets her visit her father for two months if she promises to return.
After dreaming the Beast is dying, Beauty returns to the palace and finds him weak in the garden.
Beauty agrees to marry the Beast, the enchantment breaks, and he becomes the Prince.
The Fairy summons Beauty's family, and Beauty and the Prince are married with great joy.
